name: Balkan Folk Magic
description: Balkan Folk Magic, a vibrant tapestry woven from ancient Slavic traditions and localized beliefs, represents a system of magical practices deeply embedded in the daily lives of people across the Balkan Peninsula. Rooted in pre-Christian Slavic paganism, it absorbed influences from neighboring cultures, including Roman, Ottoman, and various Balkan ethnic groups, resulting in a diverse and syncretic magical landscape. This magic encompasses a wide range of activities, from healing and protection rituals to divination and love spells. It relies heavily on the power of words (incantations, charms), natural elements (herbs, water, fire), and the intercession of spirits and deities. A crucial aspect is the concept of 'uroci' (evil eye) and methods for protection against it. Divination practices include molybdomancy (lead pouring), scapulimancy (reading shoulder bones), and coffee ground reading. The magic is often performed by individuals within the community, such as wise women ('baba', 'vestica') or healers, who possess specialized knowledge and skills. The belief in the interconnectedness of the natural and supernatural worlds is central, and rituals are often performed to maintain balance and harmony. Family and community are important, magic is often performed for the benefit of the group. It is a pragmatic magic, focused on solving everyday problems and improving life circumstances.
subtitle: Slavic Rooted Magic of the Balkans

keyMyths.title: The Origin of Uroci (Evil Eye)
keyMyths.description: The myth explains how envy and ill-will can manifest as a harmful force, causing misfortune and illness. Specific rituals and amulets are used to ward off the effects of the evil eye.

keyMyths.title: The Power of the Vila (Fairy)
keyMyths.description: Vila are powerful female spirits who inhabit forests and mountains. They can be benevolent or malevolent, and interacting with them requires caution and respect to avoid their wrath.

sources.author: T. Vukanović
sources.description: A comprehensive study of magical beliefs and practices in the Balkans.
sources.text: A comprehensive study of magical beliefs and practices in the Balkans.
